div.head: The Minister Resident in Iraq (Knabenshue) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 52. Referring to my telegram No. 51 of March 25. The Minister for Foreign Affairs also said that he had told Eden that the Iraqi Government desires the British to implement the White Paper in respect to PalestineBritish Cmd. 6019: Palestine, Statement of Policy, May 1939. but in view of present world conditions would be satisfied to leave the time of implementation to the discretion of the British.

As regards Syria he told Eden that Iraq would raise no objection to the occupation of Syria by the BritishFor correspondence on this subject, see pp. 725 ff. if for strategical or other reasons the British should deem such a step necessary—Iraq having faith and confidence that after the war the British would find a satisfactory solution for the Syrian problem on the basis of Syrian independence. In answer to a question in the Chamber of Deputies he expressed Iraq’s sympathy with the Syrians in their present attitude towards the French.
